---
title: What I've been up to
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
toc-location: left
listing:
  - id: listing
    template: reading/custom.ejs
    contents:
      - reading/items.yml
    include:
      type: "*"
    sort: "date_read desc"
    page-size: 5
    date-format: "YYYY"
comments: false
---

<!-- Somehow these are required to make icons work in the EJS template. -->
<!-- {{< fa brands github >}} -->

## What I've been reading

This is a short selection of things I've been reading, watching, or listening to.

::: {#listing}
:::

```{r}
#| include: false

library(jsonlite)
library(fs)
library(tidyverse)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  include = FALSE
)

items <- fromJSON("reading/readings.json")$items |>
  filter(itemType != "note") |>
  tibble()

# Wrangle Zotero-exported Better BibTeX JSON file to a tibble
dat <- items |>
  # Crazy hack to harmonize different author table formats
  mutate(
    creators = map(
      creators,
      ~ if ("name" %in% names(.x)) {
        mutate(
          .x,
          lastName = str_split_1(name, " ")[2],
          firstName = str_split_1(name, " ")[1],
          name = str_c(firstName, lastName, sep = " ")
        )
      } else {
        mutate(
          .x,
          name = str_c(firstName, lastName, sep = " ")
        )
      }
    )
  ) |>
  mutate(
    type = itemType,
    title = title,
    abstract = abstractNote,
    date, dateAdded, dateModified, accessDate,
    url,
    authors = map_chr(
      creators,
      ~ knitr::combine_words(.x$name, and = " & ")
    ),
    extra,
    .keep = "none"
  )
```

```{r}
#| eval: true

path <- "reading/items.yml"

# Note the Zotero 'Extra' field must contain items like
# license: CC-BY
# data: <URL>

dat |>
  mutate(extra = ifelse(is.na(extra), "extra: ~", extra)) |>
  str_glue_data("
- title: >-
    {title}
  url: {url}
  date: {date}
  date_read: {dateAdded}
  type: {type}
  authors: |
    {authors}
  abstract: |
    {str_remove_all(abstract, '\\n')}
  {str_replace(extra, '\\n', '\\n  ')}
") |>
  cat(file = path, sep = "\n")
```
